reforms.description: The Enterprise Allowance Scheme was introduced to allow individuals receiving unemployment benefits to capitalize twelve months' payments into a lump sum to start a business, promoting self-employment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encourages entrepreneurship and the creation of new businesses, which can lead to job creation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The annual uptake of this Scheme has been over 5,000.
reforms.importance_rationale: The scheme represents a significant effort to stimulate self-employment and entrepreneurship in response to high unemployment rates.
reforms.source_quote: The Enterprise Allowance Scheme introduced in 1983 permits those in receipt of unemployment benefits to capitalise twelve months' payments into a lump sum in order to start up a business.
